key_points: Deserts have low rainfall, high temperatures, and scarce vegetation
Plants and animals have unique adaptations to survive harsh conditions
Human communities rely on water conservation, wells, and irrigation
Nomadic life is common; some communities settle near oases
Trade, crafts, and festivals are important cultural activities
examples: Thar Desert in India with camels and hardy vegetation
Bishnoi and Raika communities practicing seasonal migration
Houses with thick walls, small windows, and ventilation for cooling
Camel fairs and desert folk art showcase local culture
formulas: Desert → Region with extreme temperature and minimal rainfall
Xerophytic Adaptations → Plants conserve water through leaves and roots
Nomadic Lifestyle → Seasonal movement in search of resources
Water Management → Wells, tanks, and irrigation for survival
exam_tips: Highlight how humans and animals adapt to desert life
Use specific examples of desert communities and their practices
Remember the role of water sources and trade routes
Include festivals, crafts, and cultural aspects in answers
